methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals institutional commitment to broad community-based AI education beyond elite tech circles, potentially building distributed fluency through accessible training pathways in the capital region.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in coal-transition Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capacity building in an underserved region, testing whether peer networks can distribute AI capability beyond traditional tech hubs.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying AI-powered home search tools demonstrates platform-mediated adoption spreading beyond tech sectors into traditional industries, creating shallow but distributed AI exposure through consumer-facing interfaces.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI trainer emphasizing worker-led technology adoption in Southwest/Roanoke signals potential for deep engagement rather than passive consumption, though success depends on whether training reaches beyond early adopters to build true distributed fluency.

signals.title: Virginia’s AI Data Center Gravy Train Shows Signs of Slowing - Bloomberg Law News
signals.signal_reading: Data center growth slowdown in NoVA amid tax policy debate reveals concentration of AI infrastructure investment in the tech corridor, with environmental and fiscal tensions potentially constraining the very concentration that defines regional advantage.
